feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Restriction Field: User Group
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I create a role for the administrator of company 5140 to manage users belonging to company 5140.
Setup:

  • Business Role ID: ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR
  • The values ​​of Access Categories are Restricted.
  • The values of User Group field value are ZCB5140_USER. User Group is Leading Restriction.
  • Other field values are blank.

I think the User Group field supports displaying or changing user data.

The administrator with the role ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR can do the following (based on what I've tried):

  • View all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)

 

  • Edit user data belonging to User Group ZCB5140_USER
  • Cannot edit user data belonging to other User Groups

Question:

  • Why can this administrator view all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)
  • Is my role setup correct?

Thank you very much.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content:
Olá,

Gostaria de entender o processo para criar automaticamente um pedido de venda a partir de um contrato de venda de serviços. Tenho tentado, mas não obtive sucesso.

Qual seria a melhor abordagem para gerar automaticamente o pedido de venda e a fatura mensal a partir de um contrato de venda de serviços?

Cenário no SAP Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: How to validate manually entered batch numbers during batch creation or saving in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I am looking for a way to validate manually entered batch numbers during the batch creation or saving process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
Could you please advise if there are any available enhancements, BAdIs, or extensibility options (such as Custom Logic) to achieve this validation?
Any insights, configuration steps, or relevant documentation links would be highly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Withholding Tax calculation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have quite a trouble understanding the logic (or probably we have missed a set up) that caused this computation. 

This is from two items in the billing document, and I am now aware that the SD and FI conditions/computations are not the same. However, I would like to know how the "Taxes on Sales/Purchases Amount" was derived or computed from or is it a configuration set up. The taxes on sales applied to this transaction is 12% but here the amount shows about only 2% (for both items) though they have two different WHT tax types assigned to each item. 

Can someone explain the logic/computation behind this? 

The sample expected computation is: 

Gross amount                       230,777.12
Less Taxes on Sales            - 24,726.12 (12% of 206,051)
Net amount                           206,051.00
Withholding Tax Rate                       20%
Withholding Amount           41,210.20

Also, as I was simulating another scenario trying to understand the computation logic, I created a transaction that had 230,777.12 as the gross amount still, upon simulation, the gross amount shows 1,000 (I have condition record PPR0 set up for 1,000 for this but I changed the amount in the transaction to 230,777.12).

I would like to know how the system derives these amounts. 
Thank you for your patience and help!

entry.title: Badi to Update/Change Goods Receipt Quantity in MIGO Screen
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Based upon client requirement Is there any Badi to Update or Changes Goods Receipt Quantity at MIGO Level.

Regards,

entry.title: Business Context for adding a CFL fields in MIGO Transaction Header Level in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are trying to add CFL Fields in MIGO Transaction Header Level, but unable to find the exact Business context. The business context which we getting is related to item level.

So please provide proper business context to add CFL Fields in MIGO Transaction header level.

Regards,

Vaishnavi.

entry.title: Sales Performance Plan vs Actual
entry.content: I want to add a field that calculates the percentage of planned and actual quantity , what i found was that i can add a custom field but then where will i add the calculation to it

entry.title: Consumption Tax (Input/Output VAT) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Complete Posting Flow from Invo
entry.content:
Consumption Tax (Input/Output VAT) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Complete Posting Flow from Invoice to Clearing — Which SSCUI Configures What?

**Product:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2508 / 2602 / 2608)
**Area:** Finance — General Ledger / AR / AP / Tax
**Country/Region:** Japan (JP), Chart of Accounts **YCOA**

---

## Background

I'm an FI consultant currently running Fit-to-Standard workshops for a Japanese customer on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The customer asked what I thought was a simple question:

> "Walk us through every journal entry involving consumption tax, from invoice creation through to clearing. And tell us exactly where each one is configured."

I couldn't find a single consolidated source for this, so I pieced it together myself. Below is what I've established. I'd appreciate corrections and answers to the open questions at the end.

A recurring misconception I want to address up front, because I initially fell for a variant of it myself:

> **Consumption tax does NOT move at payment clearing.**

There is no "behind the scenes" tax transfer at clearing the way there is with, say, prepaid expense amortisation. Japan taxes on an accrual basis, so SAP finalises the tax amount at invoice posting and the balance simply sits on the balance sheet until it is manually transferred at declaration time.

---

## 1. Summary Table

| # | Event | Tax posted? | Accounts involved |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Down payment **request** (Sp. G/L ind. **F**) | **No** — noted item, no G/L posting at all | — |
| 2 | Down payment (Sp. G/L ind. **A**) | **No in Japan** (config-dependent) | MVA/VVA mechanism exists for other countries |
| 3 | **Customer invoice posting** | **Yes** | Output tax (**MWS**) |
| 4 | **Supplier invoice posting** | **Yes** | Input tax (**VST**) |
| 5 | Incoming / outgoing payment clearing | **No** | — |
| 6 | Clearing with cash discount | Yes (tax adjustment) | Cash discount + tax adjustment |
| 7 | Foreign currency clearing | **No** (tax fixed in document currency) | Exchange rate difference (KDF) only |
| 8 | **Tax declaration transfer** | **Yes (manual)** | Output/Input tax → Tax payable |

---

## 2. Normal AR / AP Cycle

### 2.1 Output Tax — Transaction Key **MWS**

**Step 1: Invoice posting** (`Create Billing Documents` / `Create Outgoing Invoices`)

```
Dr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100
```

Determination path for SD-driven postings:

```
Condition type (MWST etc.)
→ Account key MWS
→ G/L account (Output Tax)
```

For direct FI postings, the tax code entered on the line item resolves MWS → G/L account directly.

**Step 2: Incoming payment clearing** (`Post Incoming Payments`)

```
Dr Bank 1,100
Cr Accounts Receivable 1,100
```

That's it. **Output tax does not appear.**

### 2.2 Input Tax — Transaction Key **VST**

**Step 1: Supplier invoice posting** (`Create Supplier Invoice` / `Create Incoming Invoices`)

```
Dr Purchases / Expense 1,000
Dr Input Tax (VST) 100
Cr Accounts Payable 1,100
```

**Step 2: Outgoing payment clearing** (`Post Outgoing Payments` / `Manage Automatic Payments`)

```
Dr Accounts Payable 1,100
Cr Bank 1,100
```

Again — **no tax movement.**

---

## 3. The Exceptions (where tax *does* move outside invoice posting)

### 3.1 Down Payment Tax Clearing — **MVA / VVA**

This is the mechanism people are usually thinking of when they intuit "there must be an offsetting tax account somewhere."

**First, an important distinction:**

A **down payment request** (Sp. G/L indicator **F**) is a **noted item**. Per SAP Help: it functions as a reference document for the down payment posting or as a document for the dunning program, and **account balances are not updated**. Neither transaction figures nor account balances are managed on these accounts — you can only view them via line item display.

So a DP request posts **nothing at all** — not tax, not receivables. This is standard behaviour worldwide, not a Japanese specialty. A tax code *is* entered and carried forward to the subsequent down payment posting, but nothing hits the G/L.

**The actual down payment** (Sp. G/L indicator **A**) is where the tax clearing account appears — in countries where a down payment triggers a VAT liability (Germany, Russia, Ukraine etc.):

```
[Step 1] Down payment received
Dr Bank 1,100
Cr Down Payment Received (Sp.G/L A) 1,100
Dr Output Tax Clearing (MVA) 100 ← the offsetting account
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100

[Step 2] Final invoice
Dr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100 ← full amount

[Step 3] Down payment clearing
Dr Down Payment Received 1,100
Cr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Dr Output Tax (MWS) 100
Cr Output Tax Clearing (MVA) 100 ← reverses Step 1
```

Net result: output tax = 100 (final invoice only). The clearing account is a **buffer that prevents double counting**.

| Key | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **MVA** | Output tax clearing on down payments |
| **VVA** | Input tax clearing on down payments |

**For Japan:** down payments received/made are **outside the scope of consumption tax** — tax is triggered by the transfer of assets or provision of services, i.e. at the final invoice. So MVA/VVA are not used. You suppress the tax posting by leaving the **tax category blank on the down payment alternative reconciliation account**, or by assigning a **non-taxable tax code** to the DP request.

SAP explicitly supports the latter pattern — see the Help topic *"How to Set Deviating Tax Codes in Down Payment Requests for Intracommunity Supply"* (ensures no output tax on the DP request while the clearing invoice is taxed with a different code). It's written for EU intracommunity supply, but the "no tax at DP, full tax at final invoice" logic maps directly onto the Japanese requirement.

**Gotcha:** if the DP reconciliation account has tax category `*`, entering a tax code will fail with an account determination error (`FH145` family) unless MVA/VVA are maintained.

**Resulting Japanese flow:**

```
① DP request (F) → no posting (noted item only)
② DP received Dr Bank 1,100 / Cr Down Payment Received 1,100 ← no tax
③ Final invoice Dr AR 1,100 / Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax 100 ← full amount here
④ DP clearing Dr Down Payment Received 1,100 / Cr AR 1,100 ← no tax
```

### 3.2 Cash Discount

Per SAP Help, the G/L account configured for cash discount expense/revenue **controls whether a tax adjustment is made**.

```
Dr Accounts Payable 1,100
Cr Bank 1,078
Cr Cash Discount Received 20
Cr Input Tax (VST) 2 ← tax adjustment
```

Rarely used in Japanese business practice, but if used, tax **does** move at clearing. Note also that reversing a clearing where a cash discount triggered a tax adjustment requires a clearing reset — the procedure is more involved than a plain reversal.

### 3.3 Deferred Tax — **not applicable to Japan**

Per SAP Help: in some countries tax is not recognised at invoice issue but only when payment is made; this is called deferred tax, and the system can record and transfer it (dedicated "Deferred Tax Transfer" app).

This is the one genuine "tax moves at clearing" mechanism — used for cash-basis countries (Italy, France, Thailand). **Japan is accrual-basis, so it does not apply.**

### 3.4 Non-Deductible Input Tax — **NAV / NVV**

| Key | Behaviour | Account determination |
|---|---|---|
| **NAV** | Non-deductible input tax posted to a **separate account** | Required |
| **NVV** | Non-deductible input tax **distributed to the expense/asset** | **Not required** |

Per KBA 3263224: NVV is not available in SSCUI 100297 by design — the tax is calculated technically but is not posted to a separate account, so no account determination is needed.

```
[NVV]
Dr Expense 1,100 ← tax absorbed into cost
Cr Accounts Payable 1,100
```

This trips people up — the absence of NVV in 100297 looks like a config gap but isn't.

### 3.5 Foreign Currency / Write-Off

- **FX clearing:** tax is fixed in document currency; only exchange rate difference (KDF) posts. No tax movement.
- **Write-off:** loss posts, but consumption tax adjustment is handled separately (in Japan, bad-debt VAT relief is a declaration-level adjustment).

---

## 4. Tax Declaration Transfer — the actual "tax clearing"

Per SAP Help (*Reporting to the Tax Authority*), there are two methods to credit the tax accounts by transferring amounts to a tax payable account:

**Method 1 — via the Advance Return for Tax program**

The `Advance Return for Tax` job creates a batch input session for automatic transfer posting of tax payable. Per Help: when the session is created, one document is prepared per selected country code, containing one item posting to the tax account and one item posting to the tax payable account, for each tax account.

The tax payable account for this is configured in **SSCUI 100297**:

```
Area : Financial Accounting
Subarea : General Ledger Accounting
Process : Define Tax Accounts
→ Step 2
Transaction Group : STE (Miscellaneous Taxes)
Transaction Key : UMS
Chart of Accounts : YCOA
→ Step 3 → assign the Tax Payable G/L account
```

(This exact path is documented in the Help topic *"Making Additional Settings for VAT Return"* — written for Ukraine, but the SSCUI navigation is generic.)

**Method 2 — manual posting via `Post Tax Payables` (App ID **F1597**)** ← *the approach we're adopting*

Per SAP Help: you use this app to pay tax amounts by posting the balances of input and output tax accounts to a tax payable account. **The app does not require entry of a tax code**, enabling direct manual adjustments to tax accounts.

Key features: save a draft before posting; post tax account balances to a tax payable account; add attachments and notes explaining the reason for the posting.

```
Dr Output Tax 1,000
Cr Input Tax 700
Cr Tax Payable 300 ← amount due
```

### ⚠️ 4.1 F1597 account visibility — the biggest trap I found

Per SAP Help, the settings in `Manage G/L Account Master Data` (SSCUI **150001**) determine whether a G/L account can be found in the `Post Tax Payables` app. **The search result does NOT include the account if any of the following apply:**

- the account is blocked for posting
- the reconciliation type has a value
- **the account is "only automatically posted"**
- the account is not "only automatically posted" **and the tax category has a value other than `>` or `<`**

So to use F1597 on your tax accounts:

| Setting | Required value |
|---|---|
| Blocked for posting | No |
| Reconciliation type | Blank |
| **Only automatically posted** | **Unchecked** |
| **Tax category** | Output tax = `>` / Input tax = `<` |

**This is a direct conflict with common practice.** Tax accounts are usually flagged "only automatically posted" to prevent manual entry — but doing so hides them from F1597. If you want F1597, you must uncheck it, which means the accounts become manually postable from journal entry apps. **Control has to be handled via authorizations (business roles/catalogs) instead.** Worth raising with the customer's internal controls team early.

Also per Help:
- **The tax payable account itself must NOT be tax-relevant** (tax category blank).
- **The tax amounts are not included in the advance report for the following month**, since the generated journal entries contain no tax information. So no double counting — this one is reassuring.

### 4.2 Japan VAT Return

```
Reporting entity : JP_RPG_ENT (Japan Reporting Entity)
Report category : JP_VAT_RETURN (Japan VAT Return)
App : Run Statutory Reports
```

Reporting activities: **`Manage Tax Items`** → **`Prepare VAT Return (Japan)`**.

**Notably, `Tax Payable Posting` is NOT among the reporting activities defined for Japan** — unlike, for example, the Italian or Ukrainian VAT returns, where it is. This appears to confirm that for Japan, SAP expects the tax payable transfer to be done manually via F1597 or a manual journal entry.

**Prerequisite that's easy to miss:** per Help, make sure the **tax reporting date** of financial documents is activated at company code level in the `Enter Global Parameters` configuration activity. For legacy financial documents where the tax reporting date is empty, **you must enter the date manually** — relevant if you're migrating open items via the Migration Cockpit.

---

## 5. SSCUI Map

| # | What | SSCUI / Activity | Location | On-prem equivalent |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Tax codes** | **101016** — Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases | CBC | FTXP |
| 2 | **Tax accounts (MWS/VST/NAV/ESA/ESE)** | **100297** — Automatic Account Determination | Area: Financial Accounting → Subarea: **General Ledger Accounting** → Process: **Define Tax Accounts** → Transaction Group **%TX** | OB40 |
| 3 | **Tax payable account (UMS)** | **100297** | as above, Transaction Group **STE** → Key **UMS** | — |
| 4 | **DP tax clearing (MVA/VVA)** | **100297** *(assumed — see Q1)* | assumed under Subarea: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable | **OBXB** |
| 5 | **DP alternative recon. accounts** | SSCUI (Define Recon. Accounts for Customer/Supplier Down Payments) | — | OBXR / OBYR |
| 6 | **G/L master (tax category, auto-post flag)** | **150001** — Manage G/L Account Master Data | — | FS00 |
| 7 | **Tax reporting date activation** | Enter Global Parameters | Company code | OBY6 |
| 8 | **VAT return setup** | Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting / Configure Rules for VAT Return | JP_RPG_ENT / JP_VAT_RETURN | — |

### Transaction key reference

| Group | Key | Description | Account determination |
|---|---|---|---|
| %TX | **MWS** | Output tax | Required |
| %TX | **VST** | Input tax | Required |
| %TX | **NAV** | Non-deductible input tax (separate account) | Required if used |
| %TX | **NVV** | Non-deductible input tax (distributed to cost) | **Not required — by design** |
| %TX | **ESA / ESE** | Acquisition tax (EU) | Not needed for JP |
| **STE** | **UMS** | Tax payable transfer | Only if using automatic transfer |
| — | **MVA** | Output tax clearing on down payments | Not needed for JP |
| — | **VVA** | Input tax clearing on down payments | Not needed for JP |

### 100297 navigation (worked example, per KBA 3016325)

```
1. Open Fiori app "Manage Your Solution"
2. Search for 100297 (Automatic Account Determination) → Configure
3. [Step 1] Area : Financial Accounting
Subarea : General Ledger Accounting
Process : Define Tax Accounts
4. [Step 2] Transaction Group : %TX (Tax on Sales/Purchases)
Transaction Key : VST
Chart of Accounts : YCOA
Tax Code : (your code)
Country : JP
5. [Step 3] Add → enter G/L account
6. Review → Save
```

**Tip:** the Step 3 screen has an "Export to Excel" option — it exports all entries for the selected category even if nothing was changed, which is useful for design document evidence. There's also a released CDS view `I_TRANSACTIONTYPESTDGLACCT` that can be used as a base for a custom view to extract current settings (I haven't verified this myself).

### YCOA accounts

The only YCOA account number I can cite with confidence is **12600000 (Input Tax)**, which appears as the worked example in KBA 3016325. I've deliberately left the others as placeholders rather than guess — see Q4 below.

| Purpose | Transaction key | YCOA account |
|---|---|---|
| Input Tax | VST | **12600000** (per KBA 3016325) |
| Output Tax | MWS | *(to be confirmed)* |
| Tax Payable | UMS | *(to be confirmed)* |
| Non-deductible input tax | NAV | *(to be confirmed)* |

---

## 6. Common Pitfalls (summary)

1. **Create a tax code in 101016 but forget 100297** → `Error in account determination: table T030K key YCOA MWS JP` (see KBA 3339321).
2. **Expecting NVV in 100297** → it's absent by design (KBA 3263224).
3. **Tax accounts flagged "only automatically posted"** → invisible in F1597 (§4.1).
4. **Tax payable account flagged tax-relevant** → rejected by F1597.
5. **Tax reporting date not activated / empty on migrated documents** → items won't appear in the VAT return.
6. **DP reconciliation account with tax category `*` in Japan** → account determination errors for unused MVA/VVA.
7. **Telling the customer the tax settlement is automatic** → it isn't. Set this expectation in Fit-to-Standard, not in UAT.

---

## 7. Open Questions

I'd be grateful for input on the following:

1. **What is the exact SSCUI path for MVA / VVA in Public Edition?**
On-prem it's `OBXB` (SPRO → FI → AR/AP → Business Transactions → Down Payments Made/Received → Define Account for Tax Clearing). I could not find documentation confirming which Subarea/Process exposes these in SSCUI 100297. I've assumed *Subarea: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable*. Has anyone maintained these in Public Edition and can confirm?

2. **Can `Tax Payable Posting` be added as a reporting activity to JP_VAT_RETURN?**
Is the activity list fixed by SAP-delivered content, or can it be extended via `Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting`? If it can be added, that would provide an automatic transfer path for Japan.

3. **How are others controlling F1597 access without the "only automatically posted" flag?**
Is business role segregation the only lever, or has anyone found a cleaner approach to protecting tax accounts from manual entry while keeping F1597 usable?

4. **Can anyone confirm the standard YCOA account numbers** for Output Tax (MWS), Tax Payable (UMS), and Non-deductible Input Tax (NAV) in the current Japan Best Practices content? I only have 12600000 for Input Tax from KBA 3016325.

5. **Reduced rate (8%) vs standard rate (10%) — separate G/L accounts?**
100297 allows account determination per tax code, so splitting is technically possible. But since the VAT return aggregates by tax code, I've been advising customers that separate accounts aren't necessary. Has anyone implemented separate accounts, and what drove that decision?

---

## References

**SAP Help Portal — S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition**
Post Tax Payables (F1597) · Advance Return for Tax · Reporting to the Tax Authority · Making Additional Settings for VAT Return · VAT Return (Japan) · Configuration for VAT Return (Japan) · Down Payment Requests · Post Customer Down Payment Requests · Deferred Tax · Cash Discount in Finance · Automatic Postings During Clearing · How to Set Deviating Tax Codes in Down Payment Requests for Intracommunity Supply

**SAP Notes / KBAs**
3009061 (tax code creation, SSCUI 101016) · 3016325 (assigning G/L to tax codes, SSCUI 100297) · 3339321 (T030K account determination error) · 3263224 (NVV in SSCUI 100297) · 2987771 (checking automatic account determination for a G/L account)

---

*Corrections welcome — particularly on the open questions above. Happy to update this post with confirmed answers so it's useful as a reference.*

entry.title: Material BOM Migration (2602) - BOM Item Document Assignment - Error 29 356 although complete docume
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am currently migrating Material BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Release 2602) using the Migration Cockpit. 
I am trying to migrate a BOM item with Item Category = D (Document).

Issue Description

The referenced Document Info Record (DIR) already exists in the system and can be assigned manually to the BOM without any issue. 
However, during the Migration Simulation, the process fails with the following error:

  • Message Class: 29
  • Message Number: 356
  • "Please enter complete document key"

Document Information

The document exists in the system with the following key:

  • Document Type: ZBO
  • Document Number: 10000000003
  • Document Version: A
  • Document Part: 000
  • Status: H3 (Released)

Migration Template Details

BOM Item

  • BOM Usage = 1
  • Alternative BOM = 2
  • Item ID = 00000002
  • Item Category = D

BOM Item Document Assignment

The following fields are maintained:

  • Material Number
  • Plant
  • BOM Usage
  • Alternative BOM
  • Item ID = 00000002
  • Document Type = ZBO
  • Document Number = 10000000003
  • Document Version = A
  • Document Part = 000

Verification Already Performed

  • The Document Info Record exists in the system.
  • The document status is valid (H3 – Released).
  • Manual assignment of the same document to the BOM works successfully.
  • All document key fields are maintained in the BOM Item Document Assignment worksheet.
  • The Item ID in the BOM Item sheet matches the Item ID in the BOM Item Document Assignment sheet.

Question

Has anyone successfully migrated BOM document assignments (Item Category D) using the Material BOM migration object in Release 2602?

Are there any:

  • known restrictions,
  • additional mandatory fields,
  • formatting requirements,
  • or linkage dependencies

between the BOM Item and BOM Item Document Assignment worksheets that could trigger this error?

Since the document exists and can be assigned manually, I am wondering whether there is a specific requirement or limitation related to the migration object itself.

Any feedback or experience on this topic would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Question regarding account number requirement in SAP payment processing
entry.content:
Hi Support Team,

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to confirm my understanding of the SAP payment process and to explain a Japan-specific payment method that may be affected. I would appreciate your confirmation on the following points.

First, is my understanding correct that if the account number is blank, the payment processing in SAP will not work properly?

Next, in Japan there is a payment method called "nofu" (納付), where you take a "nofusho" (納付書 / payment slip) to a government office, convenience store, or bank counter and pay in cash. "Nofu" is a payment method that does not require a bank account.

We post the payment journal entries for these "nofu" payments in SAP; however, if the account number is blank, that posting process does not work correctly.

Could you please advise how we should handle this case?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Soma Arai

entry.title: Applicability of Alternative FYV in a 2SL Environment
entry.content:
We are currently planning the implementation of new company codes and have encountered questions regarding the Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) setup. We would be grateful if you could clarify the following points.

Current Situation and Considerations:

We are exploring the possibility of assigning a different Fiscal Year Variant (Alternative FYV) to specific new company codes, distinct from the FYV used by our existing company codes.

Our understanding is that this Alternative FYV must be assigned to a Non-Leading Ledger (e.g., 2L, 3L etc.), and not to the Leading Ledger (0L). Could you please confirm if this understanding is correct?

Our current system operates in a 2SL environment.

Questions:

Applicability of Alternative FYV in a 2SL Environment:

    • In our current 2SL environment, is it feasible to assign an Alternative FYV to a Non-Leading Ledger for a new company code?
    • We would like to clarify if a 3SL configuration is also a prerequisite for assigning an Alternative FYV to a Non-Leading Ledger when introducing a new company code.
    • <

entry.title: Inquiry on Creating or Renaming Bank Account Types in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I would like to seek your advice regarding the Account Type field in the Manage Bank Accounts app.

Currently, when creating or maintaining a bank account, we can only select from the predefined Account Types shown below, for example:

I would like to confirm the following:

  1. Is it possible to create a new custom Account Type?
  2. Is it possible to rename or modify the description of the standard Account Types (e.g., "Current Account")?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards, 

HY

entry.title: Outbound Delivery Orders – Picking in Base Unit of Measure
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using the Outbound Delivery Orders API and have encountered a problem when the unit of measure used in the Outbound Delivery differs from the unit used during picking.

For example:

  • 1 PAL = 900 KG
  • 1 bag = 25 KG

The Outbound Delivery is created for 1 PAL, but the warehouse picking is performed in bags.

If 1 bag (25 KG) is picked, the system then shows an open quantity of 0.972 PAL.

The issue is that this does not exactly represent the remaining quantity. After picking 25 KG from 900 KG, the remaining quantity should be 875 KG, which corresponds to 0.972222222... PAL. In other words, the quantity displayed in PAL no longer matches the exact open quantity because of the rounding applied during the unit conversion.

As more partial picks are performed, the situation becomes increasingly difficult to interpret and manage.

My question is:

  • Is there a standard SAP configuration or API parameter that allows the Outbound Delivery Orders API to return quantities in the warehouse/base unit of measure (for example, KG) instead of the delivery unit of measure (for example, PAL)?

The goal would be to keep the Outbound Delivery in PAL for commercial/logistical purposes while ensuring that picking transactions and open quantities are handled in the base unit of measure, avoiding rounding and unit conversion issues.

Has anyone faced a similar scenario? What approach or solution did you use?

Thank you.

entry.title: Different Billing Currency for STO and STO Return
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, we have a customer with USD maintained as the default customer currency.

Our requirement is:

  • STO Billing invoice should be in USD.
  • STO Return Billing invoice should be in INR.
  • Both are for the same customer.

Is this possible in the standard system? If not, is there any standard workaround?

Thank you.

entry.title: Issue with Multiple Forms Under a Single Output Type
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am facing an issue with output determination and would appreciate your guidance.

We have created two Form Templates under the same output type (PICK_LIST) and assigned both forms to the same output type in the Output Parameter Determination app.

Our requirement is that when the Production Order is released, both forms should be triggered and generated. However, the system is currently triggering only one form—the form template that is maintained in the first line of the Output Parameter Determination configuration.

Could you please advise whether it is possible to trigger multiple form templates under a single output type, and if so, what configuration or setup is required to achieve this?

Please find the attached screenshot for your reference

 

Thank you for your support.

Best Regards,
Kiran

entry.title: FIFO Based Customer Automatic Clearing for Non-Bank Statement Items in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content: We are using SAP public cloud. We are having customer invoice document (FI invoices) and customer payment account documents posted from interface in S/4 system. We are checking if there's way (customer clearing process) to apply customer payments to oldest open invoice (s) for a given customer assuming if payment document is not able to find the matching open invoice for the customer. I see FIFO model for bank statement processing items in public cloud ( Link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/e06a7925f954416793cd34e0e9c667a1.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com ) and J3RCALD program ( Link:

entry.title: In the field catalog of the condition Table, Which "Region" has the meaning of Supplier's Region?
entry.content:
1. REGIOZ - Destination region
  2. REGIOA - Departure region
  3. REGIO - Region
  4. WKREG - Region of Dlv. Plant   (PlantRegion)

I want to use the Supplier's Region in condition table for my Calculation Schema access sequence. Please clarify which "Region" is Supplier's Region and which Region is the Receiving/Procuring Plant's Region (as maintained in its Address of "Define Plant" in CBC / Configuration).

Thanks in advance.

SS Attached for reference.

entry.title: 咨询S4 Public Cloud 并行线系统是否支持增加自定义字段和自定义逻辑以及会否影响已经上线的公司
entry.content:
咨询 S4 Public Cloud ERP 并行线系统 是否支持增加自定义字段和自定义逻辑，如是，会否影响已经上线的公司？

伙伴计划在其他公司代码下，在并行线里做配置。

目前如下两个界面做不了单独的自定义，盼指点原因，感谢。

entry.title: 咨询S4 Cloud ERP并行线系统是否支持增加自定义字段和自定义逻辑
entry.content:
咨询 S4 Public Cloud ERP 并行线系统 是否支持增加自定义字段和自定义逻辑，如是，会否影响已经上线的公司？

伙伴计划在其他公司代码下，在并行线里做配置。

目前如下两个界面做不了单独的自定义，盼指点原因，感谢。

entry.title: Details on Billing Plan Capabilities (Future Release)
entry.content:
Question on Road Map Explorer details:

Can you confirm the actual dates and timeline on when below updates will be implemented? We have recurring issues concerning billing plan and downpayments, and we think the release below will be able to sort out some of the issues.

SAP Road Map Explorer

Can you share some details on when will this be available for use and what changes to expect?
